title: Executive Order 7329—Authorizing the Acquisition of Land Near Petersburg, Virginia, for Emergency Conservation Work

flags.justification: The Secretary of the Interior's discretion to designate an implementing agency is real but narrow and bounded — tied to a fixed $30,000 appropriation and a specific named parcel, not open-ended policy-making authority. Corrected 2026-07-04 from an original NOT_APPLICABLE mislabel: the operational test is answerable here (minor, bounded discretion exists and was assessed), so the correct status is ABSENT per the v1.1 NA-vs-ABSENT clarification.

A single, discrete, fully-specified transaction with a clear implementation chain (ECW Director -> Interior -> NPS Director requisition) — responsibility is traceable, not unclear. Corrected 2026-07-04 from an original NOT_APPLICABLE mislabel: the accountability question is answerable here (chain is clear), so the correct status is ABSENT per the v1.1 NA-vs-ABSENT clarification.
Single coherent purpose: land acquisition near Petersburg National Military Park for conservation work.
A funding/acquisition authorization has no enforcement dimension.
No incentive structure is created by a one-time land purchase authorization.
A one-time acquisition authorization does not grant ongoing authority requiring periodic review.
No existing oversight mechanism is touched.
No regulated party or compliance scheme exists in a government land-acquisition action.
A narrow, technical land acquisition tied to a fixed sum and named parcel; nothing in the text enables, legitimizes, or forecloses activity beyond the stated conservation purpose.

Fund transfer chain from the Director of Emergency Conservation Work to the Department of the Interior, 'subject to requisition by the Director of the National Park Service,' is explicit and sequential — clean coordination rather than conflicting mandates.
No exemptions, waivers, or carve-outs are created.

notes: Validation batch, drawn negative (random sample, seed 20260703, pre-registration v1.1). First-pass coding; not yet cross-coded for inter-coder reliability.
